user_query:
I’m setting up a lightweight, event-triggered workflow for my constitutional law submissions. Please create a small automation that monitors the folder and file pattern defined in config/editorial.yaml and, whenever a new submission JSON appears, processes it to generate a compliance report and an edited cover letter.

Scope and requirements for this task:
1) Use the provided config/editorial.yaml to read both the watch settings (watch.input_dir and watch.file_glob) and the editorial policy thresholds (editorial_policy.*). Do not hardcode paths or thresholds; use the config values.
2) For each new submission JSON detected (e.g., the provided input/submissions/submission_2024_001.json):
   - Extract structured data from the submission file and compute the following metrics:
     • word_count: total words across all section texts. For this task, treat a word as any token separated by whitespace.
     • citations.case, citations.statute, citations.article: counts by reference type based on the references array.
     • footnotes.count: number of footnotes.
     • footnotes.avg_words: average number of words across all footnote texts (same whitespace-based definition).
     • citation_ratio.case: citations.case divided by the sum of case+statute+article references.
   - Compare the computed metrics to the thresholds in editorial_policy:
     • compliance.case_ratio: true if citation_ratio.case ≥ editorial_policy.min_case_ratio; otherwise false.
     • compliance.avg_footnote_words: true if footnotes.avg_words ≤ editorial_policy.max_avg_footnote_words; otherwise false.
   - Write a machine-readable report to output/reports/{submission_id}_metrics.json with exactly these fields at top level: submission_id, title, word_count, citations (object with keys case, statute, article), footnotes (object with keys count, avg_words), citation_ratio (object with key case), compliance (object with keys case_ratio, avg_footnote_words), and a timestamp field ISO-8601.
   - Read the associated draft message at input/submissions/{submission_id}_cover_letter_draft.txt and rewrite it to be concise, professional, and collegial as specified by editorial_policy.rewrite_tone. Ensure the rewritten message is no more than editorial_policy.max_cover_letter_words words. Save it to output/messages/{submission_id}_cover_letter_edited.txt.
3) Implement the automation in watch mode (monitoring the configured directory and glob). For this submission, it is sufficient to process the provided JSON once and leave the watcher in place; the deliverables should be created even if only the existing file is present.
4) Log an entry to output/logs/automation.log each time a submission is processed, including the submission_id, a short summary of compliance outcomes, and a timestamp.

Deliverables to produce in the workspace:
- A runnable script or program that implements the watcher and processing logic (language of your choice) and reads config/editorial.yaml.
- output/reports/submission_2024_001_metrics.json as specified.
- output/messages/submission_2024_001_cover_letter_edited.txt respecting tone and word limit.
- output/logs/automation.log with at least one processing entry.

Notes:
- Use only the provided files as inputs. Do not call external services.
- The computation and compliance flags must reflect the thresholds from config/editorial.yaml. I will verify by recomputing metrics from the JSON and comparing them to your report and the config.
- Keep file paths exactly as specified above. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
